English (United States)
Get started
Find, organize, and share your work, all in one place
AI is coming to Dropbox, with smarter search, faster drafting and summarization, and intelligent organization.
Try Dropbox free
See what’s new
Join the over 700 million registered users who trust Dropbox
Find
Find it fast, every time
Get to the right file, fact, or message when you need it, so you spend less time searching and more time working.
Try Dropbox free
Organize
Everything in order, everyone aligned
Keep every version current and easy to manage. With synced team folders, previews for 175+ file types, and built-in AI summaries (coming soon), your content stays organized and accessible.
Learn more
Try Dropbox free
Share
Send it safely, share it fast
Share work instantly and securely with real-time syncing and large file transfers, so teams stay connected and collaboration stays seamless.
Learn more
Try Dropbox free
Security never comes second
From industry-leading encryption and tamper-proof documents to version history and recovery, Dropbox keeps your intellectual property safe and never sells your data.
Try Dropbox free
Learn more
Testimonial
"The security of knowing our information is safe."
Customer Bryan Chandler reveals how his team uses Dropbox to organize and share files securely.
Watch testimonial
Testimonial
"Seriously impressive security features"
Tech influencer Justin Tse discusses why he trusts Dropbox to keep his content safe.
Watch testimonial
Webinar
"How do you enhance security and keep productivity reasonable?"
Your company’s sharing content with collaborators all over the world. Are you in control?
Watch webinar
Dropbox empowers across industries
Construction
With Dropbox for teams, you can store, access, preview, edit, and transfer CAD, BIM, PDF, and visual content files from anywhere.
Learn more
Media
Create a flexible media workspace that connects your teams, content, and tools together.
Learn more
Technology
Product ideation to development, streamline technology workflows so you can focus on turning great ideas into products people love.
Learn more
Professional services
Make clients happier with easier collaboration, boosted productivity, and one organized place to get things done.
Learn more
Manufacturing
Design and engineering teams can easily collaborate, manage complex reviews, and share large files seamlessly.
Learn more
Education
Power student learning, faculty research, and staff operations on Dropbox Education’s secure cloud collaboration platform.
Learn more
Discover, learn, thrive with Dropbox
Article
Working Smarter: A podcast about AI and modern work
Go beyond the hype and headlines. Hear stories from real people using AI to do more of what they love about their jobs.
Read article
Article
Dropbox teams up with McLaren Racing as an Official Technology Partner of McLaren Formula 1 Team
Dropbox brings simplified sharing, collaboration, and organization to the McLaren Formula 1 Team.
Read article
Article
The best way to send videos without losing quality
Send videos exactly as you captured them—no compression, no hassle. With Dropbox, you can share large files securely and keep every detail intact.
Read article
View more resources

Dropbox Release Notes
New updates and improvements to Dropbox
April 2026
Sign in with passkeys on mobile
You can now log in to Dropbox on mobile using passkeys in addition to a password or separate 2FA options. This speeds up sign-in and reduces friction so you can access your account more securely without extra steps.
Find similar images in Dash for Dropbox
You can now view visually related images in Dash in Dropbox when opening an image, with results surfaced automatically from across your library. This helps you discover and reuse relevant assets faster so you can find what you need without relying on folders or keywords. Learn more about
Dash in Dropbox
.
March 2026
Admins set and manage user storage limits
Admins can now set custom storage limits in Settings and view or update each user’s limit directly from the Members tab. This gives admins centralized control and visibility so you can enforce usage policies and manage storage more efficiently.
Explore team storage in admin console
Admins can now visualize and drill into your team’s full folder structure in the Admin Console to see where storage is being used without joining folders. This gives admins complete visibility into storage usage so you can identify large files and manage space without manual work or permission changes.
Review and request team storage
Admins can now see team storage usage in more places, buy additional storage as an admin, or request more storage as a team member when your team is running low from Admin Dashboard. This gives teams clearer visibility and next steps so admins can act sooner and members can flag storage issues before work is disrupted.
Preview, save and share work from ChatGPT
With the Dropbox app in ChatGPT, you can easily access and preview your Dropbox files, save AI-generated content directly to Dropbox, and share links to your work without leaving ChatGPT.
Create team from shared folder
You can now create a team space directly from an existing shared folder with pre-populated invites and file transfer options. This helps you start collaborating faster with existing collaborators without rebuilding your setup.
Learn more
Admins allow automatic team approvals
Admins can now enable anyone in their company domain to join your team automatically without additional admin approval when licenses are available
here
under “request to join the team.” This removes manual approval bottlenecks so teams can grow faster with people who already work together.
Learn more
January 2026
View and manage billing from a redesigned Admin console
Admins now see a redesigned billing page in the Admin console that replaces the previous billing experience. The updated layout makes it easier to find plans, manage licenses and add-ons, update billing cycles, and take action without navigating across multiple pages. To access: Go to the Admin console, then open Billing.
Enhancement to the team storage breakdown chart
Admins can now see inactive users in the team member storage chart. This update makes it easier to see when deleted or suspended users are using significant file storage space, which can count towards the team’s overall quota. To access: Go to the Admin console, then scroll to Storage trends to view trends and usage details.
Learn more
December 2025
Upload files up to 2TB on the web and via API
You can now upload files up to 2TB on all platforms, such as mobile, on dropbox.com, or through the Dropbox API, matching our desktop upload limits.
Learn more
November 2025
Pay with Apple Pay in web checkout and billing
Dropbox now supports Apple Pay as a payment method in select web checkout and billing flows. This allows eligible users on supported Apple devices to complete purchases or start trials using Apple Pay. To access: On supported Apple devices, Apple Pay appears alongside existing payment methods during checkout and in billing settings.
View storage trends and usage breakdown in the Admin console
Admins can now see team storage usage over time and a breakdown of how storage is used across their team members. This helps admins understand where growth is coming from, identify top storage contributors, and plan future storage needs with clearer data. To access: Go to the Admin console, then scroll to Storage trends to view trends and usage details.
Learn more
Manage deleted and restored files
Admins can now view and manage files deleted by team members from a single, centralized deleted files page, with improved visibility and filtering. We also launched a new Restorations tab with clear status indicators (in progress, successful, cancelled, failed) for all users. This makes it faster to find specific deletions, reduce context switching during investigations, and take action when responding to data changes. To access: Go to the Admin console, then open Deleted Files to view and filter deleted content across the team.
Learn more
See team storage usage from browse
Admins can now view overall team storage usage directly in browse, without leaving their current workflow. This gives quick visibility into storage consumption so admins can spot trends early and take action. To access: Go to browse and look for the Team storage widget.
Learn more

hits.title: Backblaze has stopped backing up OneDrive and Dropbox folders and maybe others
Dropbox Attempts To Kill Open Source Project

analysis.name: Dropbox
analysis.tagline: Find, organize, and share your work, all in one place
analysis.value_proposition: A cloud storage and collaboration platform that helps teams store, share, sign, and organize files securely with AI-powered search and a growing suite of productivity tools.
analysis.ideal_customer: Distributed teams and knowledge workers who need secure file storage, sharing, and a connected suite of document, signature, and video review tools

features.name: cloud storage
features.description: Store files in the cloud and access them from any device
features.evidence_quote: 2,000 GB of storage ... Starts at 3,000 GB for the team

features.name: file sync
features.description: Sync files across all connected devices in real time

features.name: unlimited device connections
features.description: Connect any number of devices to a single Dropbox account

features.name: version history
features.description: Restore previous versions of files within a configurable history window
features.evidence_quote: Account recovery and version history: 30-day / 180-day / 1-year history

features.name: deleted file recovery
features.description: Restore files deleted within the plan's history window

features.name: large file transfer
features.description: Send large files to anyone via one-way transfer links (up to 100 GB)
features.evidence_quote: Deliver up to 50 GB ... Deliver up to 100 GB

features.name: 2tb web and api upload
features.description: Upload individual files up to 2 TB on web, mobile, and via API
features.evidence_quote: Upload files up to 2TB on all platforms, such as mobile, on dropbox.com, or through the Dropbox API

features.name: file sharing
features.description: Share files and folders via shared links or direct collaboration

features.name: shared links
features.description: Generate shareable links to files and folders

features.name: team folders
features.description: Synced shared folders for team-wide collaboration and organization

features.name: 256-bit aes encryption
features.description: Files encrypted at rest using 256-bit AES

features.name: ssl/tls encryption

differentiators: Over 700 million registered users trust Dropbox
Preview for 175+ file types natively in browser
Industry-leading encryption — never sells user data
Tamper-proof documents for intellectual property protection
Full product suite in one ecosystem: storage, signatures, video review, document tracking, fax, AI scheduling
End-to-end encryption and Advanced Key Management at enterprise tier
Dash AI teammate for cross-content search and summarization
2 TB individual file upload limit across all platforms including web and API

recent_ships.what: Sign in with passkeys on mobile
recent_ships.source: You can now log in to Dropbox on mobile using passkeys in addition to a password or separate 2FA options.

recent_ships.what: Similar image search in Dash for Dropbox
recent_ships.source: You can now view visually related images in Dash in Dropbox when opening an image, with results surfaced automatically from across your library.
Admins can now set custom storage limits in Settings and view or update each user's limit directly from the Members tab.

recent_ships.what: Team folder structure explorer in Admin Console
recent_ships.source: Admins can now visualize and drill into your team's full folder structure in the Admin Console to see where storage is being used without joining folders.

recent_ships.what: Dropbox app in ChatGPT — preview, save, and share files
recent_ships.source: With the Dropbox app in ChatGPT, you can easily access and preview your Dropbox files, save AI-generated content directly to Dropbox.

recent_ships.what: Create team from existing shared folder
recent_ships.source: You can now create a team space directly from an existing shared folder with pre-populated invites and file transfer options.

recent_ships.what: Automatic team approvals for company domain users
recent_ships.source: Admins can now enable anyone in their company domain to join your team automatically without additional admin approval when licenses are available.

recent_ships.what: Redesigned billing page in Admin Console
recent_ships.source: Admins now see a redesigned billing page in the Admin console that replaces the previous billing experience.

recent_ships.what: Inactive users visible in team storage chart
recent_ships.source: Admins can now see inactive users in the team member storage chart. This update makes it easier to see when deleted or suspended users are using significant file storage space.

recent_ships.what: 2 TB file upload limit on web and via API
recent_ships.source: You can now upload files up to 2TB on all platforms, such as mobile, on dropbox.com, or through the Dropbox API, matching our desktop upload limits.

recent_ships.what: Apple Pay support in web checkout and billing
recent_ships.source: Dropbox now supports Apple Pay as a payment method in select web checkout and billing flows.

recent_ships.what: Storage trends and usage breakdown in Admin Console
recent_ships.source: Admins can now see team storage usage over time and a breakdown of how storage is used across their team members.

recent_ships.what: Centralized deleted files management for admins
recent_ships.source: Admins can now view and manage files deleted by team members from a single, centralized deleted files page, with improved visibility and filtering.

praised: Security of knowing information is safe (customer Bryan Chandler testimonial)
Seriously impressive security features (tech influencer Justin Tse)
complaints: Security concerns: 'The Dropbox hack is real' (HN thread, 1313 upvotes)
Privacy/macOS concerns: 'How Dropbox Hacks Your Mac' (HN thread, 1037 upvotes)
Negative brand sentiment: 'Drop Dropbox' (HN thread, 1990 upvotes)
Open source hostility: 'Dropbox Attempts To Kill Open Source Project' (HN thread, 886 upvotes)
Backup compatibility: 'Backblaze has stopped backing up OneDrive and Dropbox folders' (HN, 1130 upvotes)

tiers.name: Basic
tiers.headline_inclusions: 1 user, 2 GB storage; core sync, sharing, and file access

tiers.name: Plus
tiers.headline_inclusions: 1 user, 2 TB storage, 30-day history, 50 GB transfers, PDF editing, e-signatures

tiers.name: Standard
tiers.headline_inclusions: 1+ users, 3 TB team storage, 180-day history, 100 GB transfers, Dash preview, team admin

tiers.name: Advanced
tiers.headline_inclusions: 3+ users, 15 TB team storage, 1-year history, SSO, E2E encryption, compliance, tiered admin

pricing.notes: Standard requires minimum 1 user but pricing implies team use; Advanced requires minimum 3 users. Displayed prices appear to be yearly billing rates — monthly billing rates not captured in source. Enterprise tier includes dedicated support, custom security integrations, and enterprise-grade SLAs. Signature requests capped at 3/month on all non-enterprise plans; unlimited via Dropbox Sign add-on. 30-day free trial available on paid plans.
